session.name: MPI-242 Krea2 onboarding - research + live sampler A/B closed; workflow authoring in progress

goal.original: Add Krea2 (Flux-lineage image model) to Cubric Vision end-to-end. Phase 1 = Turbo variant. User authors + proves the ComfyUI workflow; agent does research + app wiring per docs/add-model-playbook.md.
goal.current: Base t2i workflow is settled (style LoRAs + sampler config both closed). NEXT SESSION IS RESEARCH-FIRST, NOT IMPLEMENTATION: investigate additional Krea2 workflows (ControlNet, image-to-image / image editing, multi-reference conditioning) and the incoming NSFW finetune. Experiment first; decide what to implement afterwards.

completed: Live A/B (user-run): sampler + refiner config SETTLED, 6 agent theories refuted
Corrected card: VAE is qwen_image_vae (reuse vae-qwen-image dep), NOT vae-flux-ae
remaining: RESEARCH (next session): ControlNet, i2i/edit, Conditioning-Rebalance, NSFW finetune
Finish workflow graph: strip enhancement branch + ResolutionSelector, ONE Output_Image
registry.py HANDLERS prefix rule + generate_krea2.py build()
dependencies.js entries (+9 style LoRAs) + R2 upload + /mpic-compute-dep-hashes
models.js ModelDef (new type, multiStage:true, FLUX_RATIOS values, loraStrengths:['model'], enhanceRecipe:'flux')
progressStages.js entry - COUNT tqdm bar restarts LIVE per run mode

task_card.title: Add Krea2 (Flux family) - Turbo variant first, full variant later

allowed_actions: implement only after user says the workflow is ready

knowledge_preservation.authoritative_doc: docs/krea2.md (333 lines) - settled sampler config, style-LoRA table + trigger contract, resolution verdict, injection seam, and a 'Dead theories - do NOT re-propose' table.
completed: SAMPLER SETTLED: stage1 euler/beta/6/denoise1.0/eta0.5/cfg1 -> stage2 refiner euler/beta/3/denoise0.35/eta0.5, latent hand-off, no VAE round-trip. Refiner beat stage-1-alone at every setting => workflow IS multiStage.
NO shift node: comfy/supported_models.py:1828 already sets Krea2 shift=1.15. AuraFlow verified inert live.

Constraints (each isolated by single-variable run): first-order sampler ONLY; ~6 steps under beta (optimum is SCHEDULER-dependent); symmetric tapered schedule (beta > bong_tangent > simple); eta>0 load-bearing (4 confirmations).

9 style LoRAs, model-only (528 tensors, all 'transformer.' prefix, rank 32 F32). LoraLoaderModelOnly + loraStrengths:['model']. No CLIP LoRA is possible - musubi-tuner pre-caches text-encoder outputs, cannot train it.
Trigger phrase APPENDED to prompt with ', ' AFTER any enhancement. 0 = no style.

Style seam: ONE int. MpiInt 'Input_Style' -> 9x MpiMath (1.0 if a==N else 0.0) -> 9x always-present MpiLoraModel.strength_model, AND -> MpiPromptList.specific_item (VERIFIED 1-indexed, 0 bails; prompt_gen/prompt_list.py:139,175).
traps: mode:4 = BYPASS, mode:2 = MUTE in the workflow JSON. ALWAYS check before claiming a node is live - agent got this wrong once and invented two blocker bugs.
The saved .json LAGS the ComfyUI canvas. ASK THE USER TO SAVE before reading the workflow file.
Output_Image IS a valid capture title (MPI-182 alias, commandExecutor.js:1253). The add-model-playbook s8 'must be exactly Output' text is STALE.
comfyController.js:1113 targets has NO string_a/string_b -> StringConcatenate is not injectable. _inject writes EVERY matching input on a node.
RES4LYF samplers.py:340 - supplying a manual `sigmas` array does NOT bypass `denoise`; it MULTIPLIES it. Set denoise=1.0 and denoise_alt=1.0.

denoise 0.35 on stage 2 starts at sigma .329 (> stage1's .275) => partial REGENERATION, not pure polish. Refiner cannot be a user on/off toggle. Stage 2 MUST get the same conditioning (style trigger) as stage 1.
ComfyUI lists LoRAs by path relative to loras root: 'krea-2\\style\\krea2_darkbrush.safetensors' (subfoldered, BACKSLASH). Touches open MPI-198 path-heal on non-Windows local engine.
Stale model list after adding files = refresh browser. NOT a YAML bug (extra_model_paths.yaml is correct).

next_action.description: RESEARCH ONLY - user wants to experiment before deciding what to implement. Three threads, all Krea2 workflow variants beyond the settled t2i:

threads.finding_so_far: A NATIVE Krea2 ControlNet exists - no Qwen-as-base-model hop needed, contrary to the video the user saw. github.com/facok/comfyui-krea2-controlnet provides 3 nodes: 'Krea2 Control LoRA Loader', 'Krea2 Control Image Encode' (encodes the control image with the Krea2/Qwen VAE we ALREADY ship as vae-qwen-image), 'Krea2 Control Apply'. Consumes comfyui_controlnet_aux preprocessor output (Depth Anything, Canny, OpenPose, lineart, normal). Keeps ComfyUI's built-in Krea2 inference path intact. Public depth LoRA weights: huggingface.co/Patil/Krea-2-depth-controlnet. No requirements.txt seen (only __init__.py + nodes.py).
threads.to_do: Verify the 'Qwen base image then hand to Krea for last steps' approach the user saw in a video - it may be an older workaround now obsoleted by the native control LoRA. Test the native path first. If adopted: new custom_nodes dep + node_lock pin + a control-LoRA weight dep.

threads.finding_so_far: github.com/nova452/ComfyUI-Conditioning-Rebalance (also a ...ConditioningKrea2Rebalance repo - check which is canonical). 'IP-Adapter-like' per-layer conditioning weighting for Krea2 image-reference editing. High-level nodes Krea2EditRebalance / Krea2EncodeRebalance. Uses qwen3vl to process UP TO FOUR images + the text prompt (base image / style ref / subject ref in the example workflow). Files: conditioning_rebalance.py, krea2.py, ideogram4.py; pyproject.toml, no requirements.txt seen.
threads.caution: The repo ALSO advertises 'bypassing the built in quality dilution from the trained safety filter' and 'works as a means to unfilter the model'. That is a safety-filter-removal feature. Evaluate the conditioning/multi-reference capability on its own merits; do NOT ship the unfilter behaviour as a product feature. Read the source before adopting - a third-party node that patches conditioning to defeat a model's safety training is a supply-chain and product-liability consideration, not just a technical one. Raise with the user explicitly.
threads.to_do: Read the actual source (deepwiki.com/nova452/ComfyUI-Conditioning-Rebalance has an 'Example Workflow: Krea 2 Image Edit' page). Determine whether multi-reference editing can be had WITHOUT the unfilter path.

threads.finding_so_far: User says 'Coyote' is releasing an NSFW Krea2 finetune, expected 2026-07-10. Unverified - agent found nothing; search after release.
threads.to_do: Confirm the release + exact HF repo. If it is a full finetune (not a LoRA) it is a separate ModelDef + transformer weight upload. If a LoRA, it may just be a dep. Would motivate a variation workflow. Note Krea2 already has an internal safety filter (see the Rebalance repo's claims) - a finetune may or may not address that.

also_open: beta @ 4 and @ 5 - the 6-step optimum is only bracketed from ABOVE (8 distorts). 5 may tie and be cheaper.
Native 2K single-pass: Turbo is spec'd 1024-2048 native (Raw <=1024). Untested. If Krea2 holds structure at 1536/1920 the hi-res question may be moot.

Whether core ComfyUI euler+beta (no RES4LYF) matches the ClownsharK config. Blocked on: plain KSampler has no eta, and eta is load-bearing. Probably cannot drop RES4LYF - but it is already a dep, so zero cost.

Krea2 detailer: 'detail' is an EXISTING op. Every Cubric detailer is LoadImage + LoadImageMask -> MaskDetailerPipe -> Output, declared per-model as workflows.detail in models.js. A Krea2 detailer = clone Chroma_detailer.json with the model stack swapped. No new op needed.

key_decisions: Prompt enhancement branch CUT from the app workflow (user tested Comfy's TextGenerate: over-expands, drifts from intent). Cubric Prompt is the app's answer to enhancement.
ResolutionSelector DELETED - app injects width/height into EmptyLatentImage. Reuse FLUX_RATIOS VALUES on the ModelDef (orientation mode). Do NOT set type:'flux' to inherit the table.
Style system: user's single-int design beat the agent's proposed object-injection seam. App injects ONE value: Input_Style (int 0-9).
Refiner ships => workflow is multiStage (needs _stage2 file + progressStages entry, 9 NFE vs 6).
Krea2 is Flux-lineage in ARCHITECTURE ONLY. Conditioning + VAE stack is Qwen. Reuse vae-qwen-image; qwen3vl_4b_fp8_scaled is a NEW upload.
context.collaboration_note: User owns workflow authoring and sets knob values live; agent does structural edits, research, and app wiring. The user's live A/B beat the agent's reasoning six times this session - test the user's instinct FIRST. See feedback_test_user_instinct_first.

resume_prompt: You are resuming MPI-242 (add Krea2 to Cubric Vision) on branch 1.2.0. Read .agents/mpi-kanban/state/handoffs/618e771a-2123-4298-9160-f7019d73e6d0.json, then docs/krea2.md. The base t2i workflow is SETTLED (style LoRAs + sampler config both closed - do not re-litigate; docs/krea2.md has a 'Dead theories' table). This session is RESEARCH-FIRST, not implementation: investigate Krea2 ControlNet (a NATIVE control-LoRA path exists at facok/comfyui-krea2-controlnet - the 'use Qwen as base then hand to Krea' video approach may be obsolete), image-to-image / image editing, and the Conditioning-Rebalance multi-reference nodes (nova452 - CAUTION: that repo also advertises safety-filter removal; evaluate multi-reference on its own merits and raise the unfilter question with the user before adopting). Also check whether the 'Coyote' NSFW Krea2 finetune shipped. The user experiments live in G:\ComfyUi\ComfyUI\user\default\workflows\krea2_turbo_t2i.json and will decide when a workflow is ready to implement - do not start app wiring until told. ASK THE USER TO SAVE before reading the workflow JSON (the file lags the canvas), and check node `mode` (4=bypass, 2=mute) before claiming any node is live.
